description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with two prominent chimneys and a combination of brick and glass exterior. The front facade is distinguished by a central bay window with floor-to-ceiling glass panels, sheltered by a triangular overhang that mirrors the main roofline. The structure includes a covered side carport or porch area, and multiple rectangular windows are symmetrically distributed on the brick walls. The rear of the house showcases a large glass gable, providing ample natural light to the upper floor. The entryway is accentuated by a projecting roof and glass doors, while the overall design blends traditional and modern elements. The house appears to be a two-story structure with a solid, substantial build.
description: A two-story brick house with a steep gable roof, large bay windows, and a covered side carport. The design features a mix of traditional brickwork and modern glass elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry with a bay window, glass doors, and a triangular roof overhang.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the large glass gable, covered porch/carport, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_2: Opposite front view with the bay window and entryway, showing the roof's full length and both chimneys.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying multiple windows on the brick wall and the steep gable roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with bay windows and large glass gable
attribute_entryway_design: Central glass double doors set within a bay window, sheltered by a triangular overhang, with no visible steps or porch
